Mniodes lehmannii is a member of the tribe Mutisieae and is closely related to other plants in the Mniodes genus. The plant is typically found in dry, sandy soils and is known to form dense clusters. The classification of Mniodes lehmannii is still being researched, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Asteraceae family.

intro: Mniodes lehmannii, also known as Lehmann's Mniodes, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The bioactive constituents of Mniodes lehmannii include sesquiterpene lactones, such as lehmannin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lehmann's Mniodes is often used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and infections.
